situation: How do you currently scope and price new projects?
What's your current utilization rate across the team?
How do you track billable vs non-billable hours today?
What does your client onboarding process look like from signed contract to kickoff?
How many concurrent projects does your team typically manage?
What tools do you use for project management and client communication?
How do you handle change requests or scope creep with clients?
What's your current approach to business development — referrals, outbound, or both?
How do you measure client satisfaction or loyalty?
What does knowledge transfer look like when a team member leaves a project?
problem: Where does the most unbillable time disappear to each week?
What's the biggest challenge in forecasting revenue for next quarter?
How often do projects go over budget or over timeline?
What's the hardest part of scaling the business without linearly scaling headcount?
When a senior person is sick or on vacation, what happens to their projects?
How much time do partners/directors spend on admin vs client-facing work?
What's your biggest challenge in finding and retaining good people?
How often do clients push back on invoices or question the value they received?
Where does communication break down between your team and clients?
What's the most common reason a project goes sideways?
How much time goes into proposal writing that doesn't convert?
What visibility do you have into project profitability before it's too late?
How do you handle it when a client's expectations don't match the deliverable?
What happens when multiple clients need the same senior resource simultaneously?
How difficult is it to replicate your best processes across the entire team?
implication: If utilization stays at [current %], what does that do to your margins over the next year?
When scope creep goes unchecked, how does that affect team morale and burnout?
What's the cost of that unbillable time over 12 months across the whole team?
If you can't forecast revenue accurately, how does that affect hiring and investment decisions?
When senior knowledge walks out the door, what does that cost in ramp-up time for replacements?
If proposal conversion stays at [current %], how many hours are you spending writing proposals that go nowhere?
How does inconsistent delivery quality affect referrals and repeat business?
What happens to your growth rate if you can't solve the talent bottleneck?
If project profitability surprises keep happening, how does that affect your confidence in pricing?
How much revenue is at risk from clients who've experienced a delivery issue?
If burnout causes turnover, what's the real cost of recruiting and training replacements?
When admin work eats into billable time, what's the opportunity cost in revenue?
How does [problem] affect your ability to take on larger, more strategic engagements?
What competitive risk do you face if firms with better systems can deliver faster at lower cost?
If you can't demonstrate clear ROI to clients, how does that affect renewals and expansions?
need_payoff: If you could increase utilization by 10%, what would that mean for your annual revenue?
How would it change your business if you could accurately predict revenue 2 quarters out?
What would it be worth to cut proposal creation time in half while improving win rates?
If your team could handle 20% more clients without new hires, how would that affect margins?
How valuable would it be to know project profitability in real-time, not after the invoice goes out?
What would it mean for retention if your team spent less time on admin and more on the work they love?
If client onboarding was 50% faster, how would that impact satisfaction scores?
How would it feel to have a system that flags scope creep before it becomes a budget problem?
What would it mean for your growth if every delivery was as good as your best team's delivery?
If you could replicate your top performer's process across the whole team, what impact would that have?
